title: lithophila radicata
intro: Lithophila radicata, also known as Lithospermum radicatum, is a plant species within the borage family native to alpine and arctic regions. It is a perennial plant that grows in damp, rocky areas and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds like lithospermic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and skin conditions. Lithophila radicata can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried extract, and has been traditionally used to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of colds and fever.
